title: lamium maculatum var. hirsutum
intro: Lamium maculatum var. hirsutum, also known as spotted deadnettle, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of iridoids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Lamium maculatum var. hirsutum include its potential to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and soothe gastrointestinal problems, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
